distill_output_instruction: Write all distilled replacements in English. Preserve technical terms (URLs, file paths, commands, version numbers, migration IDs) verbatim — do not translate them.

triage.review_instruction: Review {table_path}, then run `/mempenny:memory-apply {table_path}` to execute it. The apply step creates a full backup before touching anything.

distill.action_prompt: Apply, skip, or edit the proposal?
distill.already_tight_message: This file is already tight (≤3 sentences, already forward-looking). No distillation needed.
distill.applied_message: Distilled body written. Final size: {size} bytes.
distill.cannot_compress_message: Cannot fit all load-bearing content into 3 sentences without loss. Refusing to lie with a bad distillation.

clean.first_run_intro: MemPenny stores backups before touching your memory files in {memory_dir}. Where should backups for this memory directory go?
clean.first_run_prompt: Press Enter to accept the default, or type an absolute path
clean.config_saved: Config saved to {path}. Future /mempenny:clean runs against this memory directory will use this backup folder automatically.
clean.auto_apply_note: Applying changes now. Backup will be created at {path}.
clean.backup_pruning_hint: Backups accumulate over time. Run `ls {backup_root}` periodically and delete old backups you no longer need.
clean.clusters_potential_review_note: Some files looked related but didn't reach high-confidence cluster threshold; review manually if you want to consolidate them.
clean.cluster_analysis_failed_warning: Cluster analysis did not complete — skipping Phase B. Per-file triage actions proceed normally.

restore.no_backups_found: No backups found in {path}. Nothing to restore.
restore.no_config: No config yet — run /mempenny:clean once first so MemPenny knows where your backups live.
restore.no_config_for_dir: No backup folder configured for this memory directory ({dir}) — run /mempenny:clean here first to set one up.
restore.pick_prompt: Which backup to restore? (number, or name, or 'latest')
restore.confirm_safety: Current memory dir will be moved to {safety} before restore, in case you change your mind.
restore.safety_retention_hint: Safety snapshots accumulate over time. Run `ls -d {memory_dir}.pre-restore-*` periodically to see them; delete ones older than ~2 weeks if nothing feels wrong.

nap.prompt_backup_folder: Backup folder for this memory dir?
nap.scheduled_confirmation: Scheduled: {frequency} at {time}. Uses Claude credits per run.
nap.updated_confirmation: Schedule updated: {frequency} at {time}. Uses Claude credits per run.
nap.cancelled_confirmation: Removed nap schedule for this memory dir.

errors.memory_dir_not_found: Could not locate the auto-memory directory. Please supply the absolute path.
errors.table_not_found: Triage table not found at {path}. Run `/mempenny:memory-triage` first, then pass the printed table path as the first argument to `/mempenny:memory-apply`.
errors.backup_failed: Backup creation failed. STOPPING. No files were modified.
errors.locale_missing: Locale '{lang}' not found. Falling back to English. Submit a translation PR at https://github.com/marcelopaniza/mempenny/tree/main/locales
errors.backup_folder_invalid: Backup folder path must be absolute and writable: {path}
errors.nap_time_invalid: Time must be HH:MM in 24-hour format. Got: {value}
errors.nap_no_schedule_for_dir: No nap schedule for this memory dir.
errors.backup_folder_in_tmp: Backup folder {path} is under /tmp/ or /var/tmp/, which your system clears on reboot. Your backups would be lost. Pick a path under your home directory.
errors.settings_json_symlink: Refusing to enable auto-memory: {path} is a symlink. Resolve it manually, then re-run.

warnings.memory_dir_in_tmp: ⚠ Memory directory {path} is under /tmp/ — your system clears that on reboot. Proceeding, but your memory could vanish. (Backups will still be safe if your backup folder is elsewhere.)
warnings.auto_memory_disabled: ℹ Claude Code auto-memory is currently disabled ({reason}). Without it, MemPenny still cleans your memory directory, but Claude won't load it at session start.
warnings.auto_memory_unset_env_hint: To re-enable, unset the env var first: `unset CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY` (or remove it from your shell config).
warnings.memory_dir_empty_with_auto_on: ℹ Memory directory {path} looks empty. MemPenny will set up the backup folder so subsequent runs are seamless once files start landing here.

confirmations.auto_memory_enabled: ✓ Auto-memory enabled in {path}. Future Claude Code sessions will load memory files automatically.
confirmations.auto_memory_enable_layered_warning: ⚠ Note: project or local settings still set autoMemoryEnabled to false ({paths}). The user-level enable above won't fully apply until those layers are removed or set to true.
